mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates on equities decline and financial conditions loosen, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the easing is sustained rather than a single noisy cut.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS do not produce higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS above its recent trend signals stronger consumer spending and corporate revenue momentum, which supports equity earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS breakouts above the 3-month moving average are followed by negative or statistically indistinguishable 20d SPX returns in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A one-month increase in UNRATE is a deterioration in labor demand that tightens credit quality and weakens household income expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases of 0.2 percentage points or more are not associated with below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the average forward return is positive across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and profit-sensitive demand conditions, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month changes in INDPRO fail to predict lower 60d SPX returns or if the sign remains positive in a rolling out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and PAYEMS both weaken together, the economy is losing momentum in housing-led employment and household demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling HOUST and slowing PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not materially negative.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates are deeply negative and inflation surprises dominate discount-rate support, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surplus episodes do not coincide with below-zero average 20d SPX returns, or if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS spread has no incremental predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor-market cooling regime, a rising unemployment rate should dominate other signals because it marks falling income growth and weaker consumption, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if, in periods of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS, 5d SPX returns are not negative on average or the joint signal loses significance after controlling for either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth following a period of firm retail sales indicates demand deceleration that should weigh on earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth slowdowns paired with weakening RSAFS do not lead to negative 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction is weaker than single-series benchmarks.
